assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30001.md contains '## Classification: code change request' and '## Action
Sub-task created to address this feedback.' subtask-1.md exists with Review Context referencing PR Comment ID 30001.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains '## Classification: suggestion' and the reasoning states: 'While the language uses "should," the overall framing is suggestive: the reviewer says the index "would help" and provides it as a recommendation for query performance, not as a correctness requirement.' The comment text includes 'should also add an index' and 'would help', matching the assertion's cited suggestive language.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30003.md contains '## Classification: nit' and '## Action
No sub-task created.' No subtask file references comment 30003.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30004.md contains '## Classification: question' and '## Action
No sub-task created. The underlying gap is captured by the Scope Containment FAIL verdict in the verification report.' No subtask file references comment 30004.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (trustify-backend), '## Description' (wrap UPDATE statements...), '## Files to Modify', '## Implementation Notes', '## Acceptance Criteria'. subtask-2.md also contains: '## Repository' (sdlc-plugins), '## Description' (Add explicit guidance...), '## Files to Modify', '## Implementation Notes', '## Acceptance Criteria'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence:
subtask-1.md includes '## Target PR
https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' -- PASS. However, subtask-2.md (root-cause task) has no '## Target PR' section at all. The assertion requires 'Each sub-task description' to include this section, and subtask-2.md does not.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md includes '## Review Context' with the full original comment text from reviewer-a. However, subtask-2.md has no '## Review Context' section. The assertion requires 'Each sub-task description' to include this section, and subtask-2.md does not.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), sub-task descriptions (subtask-1.md, subtask-2.md), and a verification report (report.md). No code files appear in the outputs directory. The report does not mention any code modifications being made.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments have corresponding classification files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md. The report's Review Comment Summary table lists all 4 comments (30001 through 30004) with their classifications and actions.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows 'Overall: FAIL' and contains no mention of merging the PR. No merge action or command is indicated anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains the row: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new test file with 5 test functions covering delete, 404, 409, include_deleted, and cascade scenarios. No modified or deleted test files. |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a 'Convention upgrade assessment' paragraph: 'No upgrade was applied. The Style/Conventions sub-agent could not find a matching convention in CONVENTIONS.md (content not available for the target repository) or a counted codebase pattern in the PR diff demonstrating that partial indexes are an established migration convention. Per the verify-pr skill rules, suggestions must not be upgraded based on general industry best practices alone -- concrete project-specific convention evidence is required. The suggestion remains classified as a suggestion.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30002.md states '## Action
No sub-task created.' The reasoning explains suggestive language ('would help') and the convention upgrade assessment confirms no project convention evidence supports upgrading. No subtask file references comment 30002. The report's Review Comment Summary shows '30002 | reviewer-a | Suggestion | No sub-task (no convention evidence for upgrade)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row contains: 'Eval Quality: N/A'. The full row reads: '| Test Quality | PASS | All 5 test functions have doc comments. No repetitive test patterns detected (each test covers a distinct scenario). Eval Quality: N/A. |'

assertion_results.text: The sub-task creation for comment 30001 explicitly specifies Issue Type as Sub-task — the subtask-30001.md file, the report's sub-task section, or the review classification output (review-30001.md) indicates the Jira issue is created with issueTypeName Sub-task (not as a standalone Task) to ensure parent-child hierarchy with the parent task
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md (the sub-task for comment 30001) does not contain any mention of 'Issue Type', 'issueTypeName', 'Sub-task' as a Jira issue type, or parent-child hierarchy. review-30001.md says 'Sub-task created to address this feedback' but does not specify the Jira issue type. The report says 'Sub-task created: wrap soft_delete in transaction' but does not explicitly specify that the Jira issue type is Sub-task (issueTypeName Sub-task). None of the output files explicitly indicate the Jira issue is created with issueTypeName Sub-task to ensure parent-child hierarchy.
